data.title: LG Display reports Q3 results
data.description:
• LG Display press release (NYSE:LPL): Q3 EBITDA profit in the third quarter of 2024 was KRW 1,162 billion, compared with EBITDA profit of KRW 1,287 billion in the second quarter of 2024 and with EBITDA profit of KRW 382 billion in the third quarter of 2023.
  • Revenue of KRW 6821B (+42.5% Y/Y).
  • Net loss in the third quarter of 2024 was KRW 338 billion, compared with the net loss of KRW 471 billion in the second quarter of 2024 and with the net loss of KRW 775 billion in the third quarter of 2023.
  • Operating loss in the third quarter of 2024 was KRW 81 billion. This compares with the operating loss of KRW 94 billion in the second quarter of 2024 and with the operating loss of KRW 662 billion in the third quarter of 2023.

data.title: Husqvarna AB GAAP EPS of -SEK0.27, revenue of SEK9.74B
data.description:
• Husqvarna AB (OTCPK:HUSQF): Q3 GAAP EPS of -SEK0.27.
  • Revenue of SEK9.74B (-7.3% Y/Y).
  • Organic sales decreased by 4%.
  • Cash flow from operations and investments amounted to SEK 2,892m (137). Direct operating cash flow was SEK 4,020m (1,814).
  • In October, additional cost reduction activities were announced. These include cost savings of SEK 500m.
  • Metric Financial targets: Organic sales growth 5%; Operating margin 13%; Capital efficiency 20%.

data.title: Deutsche Bank GAAP EPS of €0.81, revenue of €7.5B; on track towards 2024 guidance
data.description:
• Deutsche Bank press release (NYSE:DB): Q3 GAAP EPS of €0.81.
  • Revenue of €7.5B (+5.2% Y/Y).
  • Common Equity Tier 1 capital ratio rises to 13.8%.
  • Provision for credit losses was €494 million in the quarter, up from €245 million in the prior year quarter and up slightly from €476 million in the second quarter of 2024.
  • Financial targets and capital objectives for 2025 Financial targets: Post-tax return on average tangible equity of above 10% for the Group; Compound annual growth rate of revenues between 2021 and 2025 of 5.5 to 6.5%; Cost/income ratio of less than 62.5% Capital objectives: Common Equity Tier 1 capital ratio of approximately 13% – 50%; Total payout ratio from 2025 Deutsche Bank reaffirms its financial targets as well as the bank’s capital objectives for 2025.
  • "Our nine-month 2024 results underline Deutsche Bank’s operating strength," added James von Moltke, Chief Financial Officer.
  • "We have consistently delivered on our guidance for both revenue growth and cost discipline; our capital and balance sheet are strong, and overall loan book quality remains solid. Looking ahead, this gives us confidence that we will meet our €30 billion revenue guidance for the year 2024 and that our continued revenue momentum, cost efficiencies, capital strength and moderating credit provisions all put us on track to deliver on our 2025 goals," he added.

data.title: ShiftPixy jumps 25% on TurboScale acquisition for $150M
data.description:
ShiftPixy (NASDAQ:PIXY) stock jumped 25% on Tuesday during after hour of trading as it announced plans to acquire TurboScale, an AI technology company, for a total of $150M.

The acquisition is structured as $75M in stock and $75M in debt, reflecting ShiftPixy's (PIXY) strategy to enhance its technological capabilities within the Gig Economy sector.

The acquisition aims to integrate TurboScale's advanced GPU cloud infrastructure and AI model deployment technologies into ShiftPixy's existing platform. Following the acquisition, ShiftPixy (PIXY) intends to launch new AI-powered tools by early 2025.

The completion of this acquisition is contingent upon the negotiation of agreements, and there is no guarantee that the acquisition will finalize.

Seeking Alpha's quant rating system warns that the stock is at a high risk of performing badly due to decelerating momentum and inferior profitability.

data.title: Ipsen S.A. reports Q3 results; raises FY24 guidance
data.description:
• Ipsen S.A. press release (OTCPK:IPSEY): Q3 Revenue of €836.6M (+8.3% Y/Y).
  • Full-year 2024 guidance: Based on the strong performance in the third quarter, Ipsen has further increased its financial guidance for 2024: Total-sales growth greater than 8.0%, at constant currency (prior guidance of greater than 7.0%). Based on the average level of exchange rates in September 2024, an adverse impact on total sales of around 1.5% from currencies is expected.
  • Core operating margin greater than 31.0% of total sales (prior guidance of greater than 30.0%).

data.title: BlackBerry joins hands with ISC2 to advance cybersecurity skills in Malaysia
data.description:
BlackBerry (NYSE:BB) partnered with The International Information System Security Certification Consortium (ISC2) to enhance cybersecurity education in Malaysia.

This collaboration aims to expand the range of globally recognized training and certification programs available to cybersecurity professionals at the Cybersecurity Center of Excellence ((CCoE)) in Cyberjaya, Malaysia.

The new partnership with ISC2 extends the range of internationally certified courses available to cyber professionals at the Malaysia CCoE to help upskill and enhance capabilities to bolster defenses against ever-evolving cyber threats.

The first ISC2 certification courses are set to begin by the end of October 2024, with additional offerings such as specialized workshops planned for future months.

data.title: Asia-Pacific markets mostly rise following quiet day on Wall Street; eyes on more earnings
data.description:
Asia-Pacific markets trade mostly higher on Wednesday, diverging from the trends observed in major Wall Street indices. The impressive market debut of Tokyo Metro, a Japanese subway operator, further enhanced investor confidence.

The Japan (NKY:IND) fall -0.67% to below 3,200 on Wednesday, reversing gains from earlier in the session as caution dominated sentiment ahead of Japan’s general election this weekend. The Japanese yen past 151.5 per dollar.

A leading Tokyo subway operator raised 348.6 billion yen in its largest IPO since 2018, reportedly 15 times oversubscribed and priced at 1,200 yen apiece, making it the largest IPO in Japan since 2018.

Traders looked forward to the general election this weekend, with polls suggesting that the ruling Liberal Democratic Party could lose its majority with coalition partner Komeito.

China (SHCOMP) rose +0.79% to around 3,300 while the Shenzhen Component gained 0.3% to 10,590 on Wednesday, with mainland stocks rising for the fourth straight session as the impact of Beijing's stimulus measures take shape.

Shares of drinks maker China Resources Beverage jumped 13.5% on its Hong Kong trading debut.

Hong Kong (HSI) rose +1.70% to 20,647 in early deals on Wednesday, increasing for the second session as gains were widespread across all sectors. Traders were upbeat after some local financial institutions in China Tuesday tapped a swap facility from the PBoC to revive the stock market.

India (SENSEX) rose +0.21%

Australia (AS51) rose +0.10% to around 8,230 on Wednesday, recouping some losses from the previous session, with mining and energy stocks leading the rebound amid firmer commodity prices.

In the U.S., on Tuesday, all three major indexes ended mixed amid rising interest rate concerns and mixed earnings results.

Investors now look ahead to earnings reports from major firms such as AT&T, Boeing and Tesla on Wednesday.

U.S. stock futures declined on Wednesday as investors navigated concerns about rising Treasury yields and mixed earnings results: Dow -0.24%; S&P 500 -0.06%; Nasdaq -0.09%.

Investors now look ahead to the Fed’s Beige Book summary of economic conditions on Wednesday for updates on the central bank’s economic outlook. Market participants are also closely monitoring the upcoming US presidential election, which remains highly competitive.

Currencies: (JPY:USD), (CNY:USD), (AUD:USD), (INR:USD), (HKD:USD), (NZD:USD).

More on Asia:

Japan's inflation rate slows to 2.5% in September

China's Q3 GDP figures point at a slowing economy

Australia's unemployment rate steady at 4.1%

Japan records higher than expected deficit, exports drop first time in 10 months

data.title: ProQR Therapeutics prices $75M offering and enters $12M private placement
data.description:
• ProQR Therapeutics (NASDAQ:PRQR) priced its previously announced underwritten public offering of 18M ordinary shares at a public offering price of $3.50 per share.
  • Total gross proceeds are expected to be $63M.
  • Underwriters have a 30-day option to purchase up to 2.7M additional ordinary shares at the public offering price.
  • Concurrently with the offering, ProQR (PRQR) entered into a share purchase agreement with Eli Lilly and Company, one of its existing shareholders to purchase, ~3.52M shares for total gross proceeds of ~$12.3M.
  • Net proceeds together with its existing cash and cash equivalents will be used to primarily fund research and development and clinical development to support the advancement of its current or future product candidates and the expansion of its research and development programs, and the remainder for working capital, capital expenditures and other general corporate purposes.
  • The stock price dropped 4.8% on Tuesday during after hours of trading.

data.title: Founder Group prices IPO at $4 per share
data.description:
• Founder Group (FGL) priced its initial public offering of an aggregate 1,218,750 ordinary shares at a public offering price of $4.00 per share.
  • Total gross proceeds are expected to be $4.875M.
  • Underwriters have a 45-day option to purchase up to an additional 182,813 ordinary shares at the IPO price.
  • Founder Group is a pure-play, end-to-end EPCC solutions provider for solar PV facilities in Malaysia.

data.title: Canadian National Railway declares CAD 0.845 dividend
data.description:
• Canadian National Railway (NYSE:CNI) declares CAD 0.845/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 2.18%
  • Payable Dec. 30; to shareholders of record Dec. 9; ex-div Dec. 9.
  • See CNI Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.
